arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca told he's still not proved himself at Chelsea despite ruthless early approach
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has successfully remodelled Chelsea's bloated squad without sacrificing results at the start of the season but bigger tests are still to come, warns Emmanuel Petit

arts.ttl: Newcastle v AFC Wimbledon EFL Cup team line-ups - Pigott partners Bugiel up top with Chelsea last 16 clash up for grabs
arts.sum: Subs: Ward, Furlong, O’Toole, Kelly, Neufville, Smith, Stevens, Williams, Sasu. Joe Pigott, Callum Maycock and Huseyin Biler have all come into the team, with Josh Neufvill, Alistair Smith and Matty Stevens dropping out and named on the bench.

arts.ttl: Newcastle narrowly beat AFC Wimbledon to set up tie with Chelsea
arts.sum: Newcastle laboured to a 1-0 win over League Two side AFC Wimbledon to book their place in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup.

arts.ttl: Chelsea's nightmare seven-game fixture run as Carabao Cup opponents confirmed
arts.sum: Chelsea are set for a horror seven-match run of fixtures after the October international break after Newcastle United advanced to the fourth round of the Carabao Cup. Enzo Maresca now knows he will play the Magpies twice in three days after first meeting them at Stamford Bridge in the Premier League on October 27 before travelling to St James' Park on October 30 in the Carabao Cup .

arts.ttl: 'At this point': Frank Lampard responds when asked if current Arsenal duo compare to Chelsea legends
arts.sum: Frank Lampard was asked on Tuesday night about Arsenal’s current centre-back combination and how they compare to a legendary Chelsea partnership from the past. There’s...

arts.ttl: Ian Wright names Chelsea transfer under Mauricio Pochettino that 'won't live well in history' for rivals
arts.sum: Chelsea have spent, spent, spent and carried on spending for the whole world in recent times. Regardless of the outside noise, Clearlake Capital and Todd...

arts.ttl: Chelsea star Reece James drops 10-word injury update with return date teased
arts.sum: Chelsea captain Reece James has told supporters he will see them'soon' in the latest update on his recovery from injury. On Instagram on Tuesday night, James posted an update that included how he was dealing with his latest setback.

arts.ttl: 'It's hard to imagine': Pat Nevin makes prediction about Chelsea star Cole Palmer
